framework.name: NIST SP 800-218 Secure Software Development Framework
framework.description: The NIST Secure Software Development Framework (SSDF) is a set of fundamental, sound, and secure software development practices based on established secure software development practice documents. It organizes secure software development practices into four groups: Prepare the Organization (PO), Protect the Software (PS), Produce Well-Secured Software (PW), and Respond to Vulnerabilities (RV).
framework.verification_notes: All 4 practice groups, 19 practices, and 42 tasks verified against NIST SP 800-218 Version 1.1 (February 2022). PW.3 does not exist in v1.1 (merged into PW.4). PW.4.3 was removed. Practice codes PW.1-PW.9 include a gap at PW.3.

concepts.definition_en: The four practice groups of the SSDF organize secure software development practices: Prepare the Organization (PO), Protect the Software (PS), Produce Well-Secured Software (PW), and Respond to Vulnerabilities (RV).
concepts.definition_nb: The four practice groups of the SSDF organize secure software development practices: Prepare the Organization (PO), Protect the Software (PS), Produce Well-Secured Software (PW), and Respond to Vulnerabilities (RV).

concepts.definition_en: Ensure that the organization's people, processes, and technology are prepared to perform secure software development at the organization level and, as necessary, for each individual software development project.
concepts.definition_nb: Ensure that the organization's people, processes, and technology are prepared to perform secure software development at the organization level and, as necessary, for each individual software development project.

concepts.definition_en: Protect all components of the software from tampering and unauthorized access.
concepts.definition_nb: Protect all components of the software from tampering and unauthorized access.

concepts.definition_en: Produce well-secured software with minimal security vulnerabilities in its releases.
concepts.definition_nb: Produce well-secured software with minimal security vulnerabilities in its releases.

concepts.definition_en: Identify residual vulnerabilities in software releases and respond appropriately to address those vulnerabilities and prevent similar ones from occurring in the future.
concepts.definition_nb: Identify residual vulnerabilities in software releases and respond appropriately to address those vulnerabilities and prevent similar ones from occurring in the future.

concepts.name_en: Define Security Requirements for Software Development
concepts.definition_en: Establish and maintain security requirements across the software development lifecycle, originating from both internal policies and external regulations.
concepts.definition_nb: Establish and maintain security requirements across the software development lifecycle, originating from both internal policies and external regulations.

concepts.definition_en: Identify and document all security requirements for the organization's software development infrastructure environments and implement controls to enforce requirements.
concepts.definition_nb: Identify and document all security requirements for the organization's software development infrastructure environments and implement controls to enforce requirements.

concepts.definition_en: Identify and document all security requirements for organization-developed software to meet, and communicate requirements to software development personnel.
concepts.definition_nb: Identify and document all security requirements for organization-developed software to meet, and communicate requirements to software development personnel.

concepts.definition_en: Communicate requirements to all third parties who will provide commercial software components to the organization for operational use, and assess how well the requirements are being met.
concepts.definition_nb: Communicate requirements to all third parties who will provide commercial software components to the organization for operational use, and assess how well the requirements are being met.

concepts.definition_en: Ensure all stakeholders in the organization and, if applicable, in individual software development projects, possess the necessary skills, knowledge, and competencies to perform their secure development roles and responsibilities throughout the SDLC.
concepts.definition_nb: Ensure all stakeholders in the organization and, if applicable, in individual software development projects, possess the necessary skills, knowledge, and competencies to perform their secure development roles and responsibilities throughout the SDLC.

concepts.definition_en: Create new roles and alter responsibilities for existing roles as needed to encompass all parts of the SDLC; document the roles, responsibilities, and the necessary skills for each.
concepts.definition_nb: Create new roles and alter responsibilities for existing roles as needed to encompass all parts of the SDLC; document the roles, responsibilities, and the necessary skills for each.

concepts.definition_en: Provide role-based training for all personnel with responsibilities that contribute to secure development; periodically review personnel proficiency and update training as needed.
concepts.definition_nb: Provide role-based training for all personnel with responsibilities that contribute to secure development; periodically review personnel proficiency and update training as needed.

concepts.definition_en: Obtain upper management or authorizing official commitment to secure development, and communicate this commitment to all with software development or support roles.
concepts.definition_nb: Obtain upper management or authorizing official commitment to secure development, and communicate this commitment to all with software development or support roles.

concepts.definition_en: Use automation to reduce human effort and improve the accuracy, reproducibility, usability, and comprehensiveness of security practices throughout the SDLC.
concepts.definition_nb: Use automation to reduce human effort and improve the accuracy, reproducibility, usability, and comprehensiveness of security practices throughout the SDLC.

concepts.definition_en: Specify which types of tools are required to mitigate identified risks and define how the tools are to be integrated into the overall SDLC.
concepts.definition_nb: Specify which types of tools are required to mitigate identified risks and define how the tools are to be integrated into the overall SDLC.

concepts.definition_en: Follow recommended security practices to deploy, operate, and maintain tools and toolchains.
concepts.definition_nb: Follow recommended security practices to deploy, operate, and maintain tools and toolchains.

concepts.definition_en: Configure tools to generate artifacts of their actions and of the status of security practices.
concepts.definition_nb: Configure tools to generate artifacts of their actions and of the status of security practices.

concepts.name_en: Define and Use Criteria for Software Security Checks
concepts.name_nb: Definer og bruk kriterier for programvaresikkerhetskontroller
concepts.definition_en: Define the pass/fail criteria for security checks and track the security check criteria throughout the SDLC.
concepts.definition_nb: Define the pass/fail criteria for security checks and track the security check criteria throughout the SDLC.

concepts.definition_en: Define the pass/fail criteria for each stage of the SDLC, track and acknowledge the status of security checks for software throughout the SDLC.
concepts.definition_nb: Define the pass/fail criteria for each stage of the SDLC, track and acknowledge the status of security checks for software throughout the SDLC.

concepts.definition_en: Implement processes, mechanisms, etc., to gather and safeguard necessary information to support security checks.
concepts.definition_nb: Implement processes, mechanisms, etc., to gather and safeguard necessary information to support security checks.

concepts.name_en: Implement and Maintain Secure Environments for Software Development
concepts.name_nb: Implementer og vedlikehold sikre miljøer for programvareutvikling
concepts.definition_en: Ensure that all components of the environments for software development are strongly protected from internal and external threats to prevent compromises of the environments or the software being developed or maintained within them.
concepts.definition_nb: Ensure that all components of the environments for software development are strongly protected from internal and external threats to prevent compromises of the environments or the software being developed or maintained within them.

concepts.definition_en: Separate and protect each environment involved in software development from other environments and external threats, including separating development, testing, and production environments.
concepts.definition_nb: Separate and protect each environment involved in software development from other environments and external threats, including separating development, testing, and production environments.

concepts.definition_en: Secure and harden development endpoints (i.e., developer workstations, build servers, test servers) using risk-based hardening standards and monitoring.
concepts.definition_nb: Secure and harden development endpoints (i.e., developer workstations, build servers, test servers) using risk-based hardening standards and monitoring.

concepts.name_en: Protect All Forms of Code from Unauthorized Access and Tampering
concepts.name_nb: Beskytt all kode mot uautorisert tilgang og manipulering
concepts.definition_en: Prevent unauthorized changes to code, both to protect its integrity and to prevent malicious code from being inserted.
concepts.definition_nb: Prevent unauthorized changes to code, both to protect its integrity and to prevent malicious code from being inserted.

concepts.definition_en: Store all forms of code — including source code, executable code, and configuration-as-code — based on the principle of least privilege so that only authorized personnel, tools, services, etc., have access.
concepts.definition_nb: Store all forms of code — including source code, executable code, and configuration-as-code — based on the principle of least privilege so that only authorized personnel, tools, services, etc., have access.

concepts.name_en: Provide a Mechanism for Verifying Software Release Integrity
concepts.name_nb: Tilby en mekanisme for å verifisere integriteten til programvareutgivelser
concepts.definition_en: Help software acquirers ensure that the software they acquire is legitimate and has not been tampered with.
concepts.definition_nb: Help software acquirers ensure that the software they acquire is legitimate and has not been tampered with.

concepts.definition_en: Make software integrity verification information available to software acquirers.
concepts.definition_nb: Make software integrity verification information available to software acquirers.

concepts.name_en: Archive and Protect Each Software Release
concepts.definition_en: Preserve software releases in order to help identify, analyze, and eliminate vulnerabilities discovered in the software after release.
concepts.definition_nb: Preserve software releases in order to help identify, analyze, and eliminate vulnerabilities discovered in the software after release.

concepts.definition_en: Securely archive the necessary files and supporting data (e.g., integrity verification information, provenance data) to be retained for each software release.
concepts.definition_nb: Securely archive the necessary files and supporting data (e.g., integrity verification information, provenance data) to be retained for each software release.

concepts.definition_en: Collect, safeguard, maintain, and share provenance data for all components of each software release (e.g., software bill of materials [SBOM], source code, build scripts, build and test tools).
concepts.definition_nb: Collect, safeguard, maintain, and share provenance data for all components of each software release (e.g., software bill of materials [SBOM], source code, build scripts, build and test tools).

concepts.name_en: Design Software to Meet Security Requirements and Mitigate Security Risks
concepts.name_nb: Utform programvare for å møte sikkerhetskrav og redusere sikkerhetsrisiko
concepts.definition_en: Address security requirements and risks during design to reduce the number of vulnerabilities in the released software, limit the potential impact of exploiting undetected or unaddressed vulnerabilities, and share the security design with other relevant development teams.
concepts.definition_nb: Address security requirements and risks during design to reduce the number of vulnerabilities in the released software, limit the potential impact of exploiting undetected or unaddressed vulnerabilities, and share the security design with other relevant development teams.

concepts.definition_en: Use risk modeling (e.g., threat modeling, attack modeling) and/or attack surface analysis to establish security risk context for the software and its design.
concepts.definition_nb: Use risk modeling (e.g., threat modeling, attack modeling) and/or attack surface analysis to establish security risk context for the software and its design.

concepts.definition_en: Track and maintain the software's security requirements, risks, and design decisions.
concepts.definition_nb: Track and maintain the software's security requirements, risks, and design decisions.

concepts.definition_en: Where feasible, build in support for using standardized security features and services (e.g., authentication, access control, auditing) instead of creating proprietary implementations.
concepts.definition_nb: Where feasible, build in support for using standardized security features and services (e.g., authentication, access control, auditing) instead of creating proprietary implementations.

concepts.name_en: Review the Software Design to Verify Compliance with Security Requirements and Risk Information
concepts.name_nb: Gjennomgå programvaredesignet for å verifisere samsvar med sikkerhetskrav og risikoinformasjon
concepts.definition_en: Identify vulnerabilities before implementing the software design to reduce the cost and effort needed to address vulnerabilities and to prevent security failures.
concepts.definition_nb: Identify vulnerabilities before implementing the software design to reduce the cost and effort needed to address vulnerabilities and to prevent security failures.

concepts.definition_en: Have qualified reviewer(s) confirm that the software design meets all of the security requirements and satisfactorily addresses the identified risk information.
concepts.definition_nb: Have qualified reviewer(s) confirm that the software design meets all of the security requirements and satisfactorily addresses the identified risk information.

concepts.name_en: Reuse Existing, Well-Secured Software When Feasible
concepts.name_nb: Gjenbruk eksisterende, godt sikret programvare der det er mulig
concepts.definition_en: Lower the cost of developing well-secured software and decrease the overall attack surface by leveraging pre-existing, well-secured software that provides functionality needed by the software.
concepts.definition_nb: Lower the cost of developing well-secured software and decrease the overall attack surface by leveraging pre-existing, well-secured software that provides functionality needed by the software.

concepts.definition_en: Acquire and maintain well-secured software components (e.g., software libraries, modules, middleware) from commercial, open-source, and other third-party developers for use by the organization's software.
concepts.definition_nb: Acquire and maintain well-secured software components (e.g., software libraries, modules, middleware) from commercial, open-source, and other third-party developers for use by the organization's software.

concepts.definition_en: Create and maintain well-secured in-house developed software components using SDLC processes.
concepts.definition_nb: Create and maintain well-secured in-house developed software components using SDLC processes.

concepts.definition_en: Verify that acquired components comply with security requirements and address known vulnerabilities throughout their lifecycle.
concepts.definition_nb: Verify that acquired components comply with security requirements and address known vulnerabilities throughout their lifecycle.

concepts.name_en: Create Source Code by Adhering to Secure Coding Practices
concepts.name_nb: Lag kildekode ved å følge sikker kodeingspraksis
concepts.definition_en: Decrease the number of security vulnerabilities in the software by adhering to secure coding practices and documenting the chosen practices.
concepts.definition_nb: Decrease the number of security vulnerabilities in the software by adhering to secure coding practices and documenting the chosen practices.

concepts.definition_en: Follow all secure coding practices that are appropriate to the development languages and environment to meet the organization's requirements.
concepts.definition_nb: Follow all secure coding practices that are appropriate to the development languages and environment to meet the organization's requirements.

concepts.name_en: Configure the Compilation, Interpreter, and Build Processes to Improve Executable Security
concepts.name_nb: Konfigurer kompilerings-, fortolker- og byggeprosesser for å forbedre kjørbar sikkerhet
concepts.definition_en: Decrease the number of security vulnerabilities in the software by eliminating vulnerabilities before testing occurs.
concepts.definition_nb: Decrease the number of security vulnerabilities in the software by eliminating vulnerabilities before testing occurs.

concepts.definition_en: Use compiler, interpreter, and build tool features that can make the resulting executable code more difficult for an attacker to exploit.
concepts.definition_nb: Use compiler, interpreter, and build tool features that can make the resulting executable code more difficult for an attacker to exploit.

concepts.definition_en: Determine, implement, and use approved build configurations and document them.
concepts.definition_nb: Determine, implement, and use approved build configurations and document them.

concepts.name_en: Review and/or Analyze Human-Readable Code to Identify Vulnerabilities
concepts.name_nb: Gjennomgå og/eller analyser lesbar kode for å identifisere sårbarheter
concepts.definition_en: Find vulnerabilities in human-readable code so they can be corrected before the software is in an executable form that is harder to analyze.
concepts.definition_nb: Find vulnerabilities in human-readable code so they can be corrected before the software is in an executable form that is harder to analyze.

concepts.definition_en: Determine whether code review and/or automated analysis should be used for the software; document the decision and its rationale.
concepts.definition_nb: Determine whether code review and/or automated analysis should be used for the software; document the decision and its rationale.

concepts.definition_en: Perform code review and/or automated analysis based on the organization's secure coding standards; identify, triage, and address discovered issues and record the outcomes.
concepts.definition_nb: Perform code review and/or automated analysis based on the organization's secure coding standards; identify, triage, and address discovered issues and record the outcomes.

concepts.name_en: Test Executable Code to Identify Vulnerabilities
concepts.name_nb: Test kjørbar kode for å identifisere sårbarheter
concepts.definition_en: Detect vulnerabilities in executable code so that they can be corrected before the software is widely deployed.
concepts.definition_nb: Detect vulnerabilities in executable code so that they can be corrected before the software is widely deployed.

concepts.definition_en: Determine whether executable code testing should be performed and, if so, which types of testing should be used; document the decision and its rationale.
concepts.definition_nb: Determine whether executable code testing should be performed and, if so, which types of testing should be used; document the decision and its rationale.

concepts.definition_en: Scope, design, and perform testing using the determined types; document results and triage discovered issues.
concepts.definition_nb: Scope, design, and perform testing using the determined types; document results and triage discovered issues.

concepts.name_en: Configure Software to Have Secure Settings by Default
concepts.name_nb: Konfigurer programvare til å ha sikre standardinnstillinger
concepts.definition_en: Help protect against poor security practices by users and administrators by establishing secure defaults.
concepts.definition_nb: Help protect against poor security practices by users and administrators by establishing secure defaults.

concepts.definition_en: Define a secure baseline by determining the default security settings, document the settings, and provide guidance for changing settings.
concepts.definition_nb: Define a secure baseline by determining the default security settings, document the settings, and provide guidance for changing settings.

concepts.definition_en: Implement the secure default settings and document each setting for administrators.
concepts.definition_nb: Implement the secure default settings and document each setting for administrators.

concepts.name_en: Identify and Confirm Vulnerabilities on an Ongoing Basis
concepts.definition_en: Help ensure that vulnerabilities in software releases are identified more quickly so that they can be remediated more quickly to reduce their risk.
concepts.definition_nb: Help ensure that vulnerabilities in software releases are identified more quickly so that they can be remediated more quickly to reduce their risk.

concepts.definition_en: Gather information from software acquirers, users, and public sources on potential vulnerabilities in the software and investigate all credible reports.
concepts.definition_nb: Gather information from software acquirers, users, and public sources on potential vulnerabilities in the software and investigate all credible reports.

concepts.definition_en: Review, analyze, and/or test the software's code to identify or confirm the presence of previously undetected vulnerabilities.
concepts.definition_nb: Review, analyze, and/or test the software's code to identify or confirm the presence of previously undetected vulnerabilities.

concepts.definition_en: Have a policy that addresses vulnerability disclosure and remediation, and implement supporting roles, responsibilities, and processes for disclosing and remediating vulnerabilities.
concepts.definition_nb: Have a policy that addresses vulnerability disclosure and remediation, and implement supporting roles, responsibilities, and processes for disclosing and remediating vulnerabilities.

concepts.definition_en: Address all discovered vulnerabilities commensurate with the risks they pose to the software and its users.
concepts.definition_nb: Address all discovered vulnerabilities commensurate with the risks they pose to the software and its users.

concepts.definition_en: Analyze each vulnerability to gather sufficient information about risk to support prioritization of remediation.
concepts.definition_nb: Analyze each vulnerability to gather sufficient information about risk to support prioritization of remediation.

concepts.definition_en: Plan and implement risk-based remediations for all discovered vulnerabilities, including notifying appropriate parties about vulnerabilities and their remediation.
concepts.definition_nb: Plan and implement risk-based remediations for all discovered vulnerabilities, including notifying appropriate parties about vulnerabilities and their remediation.

concepts.name_en: Analyze Vulnerabilities to Identify Their Root Causes
concepts.name_nb: Analyser sårbarheter for å identifisere grunnårsakene
concepts.definition_en: Reduce the frequency of vulnerabilities in the future by understanding why they occur.
concepts.definition_nb: Reduce the frequency of vulnerabilities in the future by understanding why they occur.

concepts.definition_en: Analyze each reported vulnerability to determine its root cause.
concepts.definition_nb: Analyze each reported vulnerability to determine its root cause.

concepts.definition_en: Analyze the root causes over time to identify patterns (e.g., a particular practice is repeatedly not followed) and take steps to address such patterns.
concepts.definition_nb: Analyze the root causes over time to identify patterns (e.g., a particular practice is repeatedly not followed) and take steps to address such patterns.

concepts.definition_en: Review the software for similar vulnerabilities to evaluate whether they are present and proactively fix them.
concepts.definition_nb: Review the software for similar vulnerabilities to evaluate whether they are present and proactively fix them.

concepts.definition_en: Review the SDLC process, and update it if appropriate, so the root cause is less likely to recur in updates to the software or in new software being developed.
concepts.definition_nb: Review the SDLC process, and update it if appropriate, so the root cause is less likely to recur in updates to the software or in new software being developed.
